# Pattern Extensibility for KB Editor

> KB Editor (`kbbridge.genexus-visual-editor`) is the GeneXus visual editor inside VS Code /
> VSCodium. It exposes a PatternExtensionAPI that lets pattern providers add custom types
> (dynamic dropdowns), captions (node labels/icons) and custom actions (context commands) to
> the editing of GeneXus pattern instances (.gxPattern). This file indexes the documentation
> for humans and LLMs.

## Concepts

- [Introduction](01-introduction.md): the problem, the solution, and the glossary.
- [GeneXus pattern concepts](02-genexus-pattern-concepts.md): .Pattern / *Instance.xml /
  *Settings.xml, attribute types, and where custom(...) types come from.
- [Extensibility architecture](03-extensibility-architecture.md): layers, the
  activation/registration lifecycle, and graceful degradation.
- [The three mechanisms](04-extensibility-mechanisms.md): Custom Types, Captions, Custom
  Actions (plus optional Variables).
- [C# → TypeScript workflow](05-csharp-to-typescript-workflow.md): how to transcribe a
  GeneXus .NET pattern to TypeScript, with the .NET→SDK mapping.
- [Getting started](06-getting-started.md): install KB Editor, clone the starter, build,
  package, test.

## Projects (source of truth & deeper docs)

- genexus-pattern-sdk: the SDK (@kbbridge/genexus-sdk, MIT) you code against.
- kb-editor-pattern-starter: blank template; see its ai/ folder for the full build guide.
- genexus-workwith-kb-editor: complete worked example (GeneXus Work With port); see its ai/
  folder including SDK-REFERENCE.md and EXAMPLES.md.
- kb-editor-pattern-extensions: download platform/catalog of pattern extensions.

## Key reference

- SDK surface (exact interfaces): genexus-workwith-kb-editor/ai/SDK-REFERENCE.md
- Mechanism guides: ai/EXTENSIBILITY-CUSTOM-TYPES.md, ai/EXTENSIBILITY-CAPTIONS.md,
  ai/EXTENSIBILITY-CUSTOM-ACTIONS.md
